title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. groenlandica
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. groenlandica, also known as Greenland bellflower, is a subspecies of the Campanula rotundifolia plant, which is a perennial flowering plant native to the Arctic tundra. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Campanula rotundifolia subsp. groenlandica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help combat infections. Herbal preparations of Campanula rotundifolia subsp. groenlandica can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support skin health and immune function.
